info.name: Smart Assessment AI - Backend APIs
info.description: Standardized response format: all responses wrapped in {status, result}. JWT Auth, Accounts, Academics (Programs, Semesters, Courses, PLO, CLO, GA), Assessments. CLO creation blocked for inactive courses. PLO creation blocked when all program semesters are inactive. GA (Graduate Attributes) maps to PLOs — admin only write access.

request.description: Admin only. Returns: users (total/admin/teacher/student), programs, semesters (total/active/inactive), courses (total/active/inactive/theory/lab), course_assignments, PLO count, CLO count.
Returns all module permissions for the currently logged-in user. Admins get full access on all modules.

item.name: Get All Modules for a Role (by-role)

request.description: Returns all course assignments. Response now includes program_id, program_code, program_name, semester_id, semester_name for each assignment.
Returns courses assigned to the logged-in teacher. Each record now includes separate credit_hours_theory and credit_hours_lab integer fields in addition to the combined credit_hours string.

formdata.description: CSV or Excel (.xlsx/.xls). Columns: code, name

request.description: Activates semester AND sets all linked courses to is_active=True
Deactivates semester AND sets all linked courses to is_active=False
Links courses to this semester (sets semester_ref FK). replace=true unlinks existing courses first.

formdata.description:
CSV or Excel (.xlsx/.xls). Each row = ONE course (theory and lab are SEPARATE rows with separate codes e.g. CMC111 and CMC111-L).
Columns: semester, code, name, course_type (THEORY/LAB), course_class (CORE/GER/ELECTIVE), credit_hours_theory, credit_hours_lab, program_code, pre_requisites (comma-separated codes), co_requisites (comma-separated codes)
Example theory row: 1,CMC111,Programming Fundamentals,THEORY,CORE,3,0,BSCS,,CMC111-L
Example lab row:    1,CMC111-L,Programming Fundamentals (Lab),LAB,CORE,0,1,BSCS,,

request.description: Filter PLOs for a specific program
Blocked if all program semesters are inactive
Multipart form: file (CSV/XLSX). Columns: program_id (integer), plo_number, description
Permanently deletes ALL PLOs from the database. Use before re-importing.
Returns CLOs with course_name, semester, course_class, credit_hours, pre_req, co_req.
Filter CLOs for a specific course ID.

Create a single CLO.

Required:
  course_code  — code from Courses API (e.g. 'CMC111', 'CMC111-L')
  clo_number   — 1, 2, or 3
  description  — CLO description text
  bt_level     — C1-C6 (Cognitive) | P1-P5 (Psychomotor) | A1-A5 (Affective)

Optional (GA mapping — use one):
  ga_code   — string code from GA list: 'GA-2', 'GA2', or just '2' (all accepted)
  mapped_ga — GA database id (from GA list API response 'id' field)

Response includes:
  ga_code — the resolved GA code as 'GA-2' string (no raw integer id shown)

Blocked if course is inactive.
Full update. Same fields as Create CLO. For GA: send ga_code ('GA-2') or mapped_ga (GA database id).
Update only the fields you send. Supported: description, bt_level, ga_code (e.g. 'GA-3'), mapped_ga (GA database id).
Returns CLOs for a course with their GA→PLO chain.

Multipart form: file (CSV / XLSX).

Column headers (order does not matter):
  course_code | course_name | semester | course_class | credit_hours | pre_req | co_req | clo_number | description | bt_level | ga_code

Stored columns:
  course_code  — must match a course in the DB
  clo_number   — 1, 2, 3 ...
  description  — CLO text
  bt_level     — C1-C6 (Cognitive) | P1-P5 (Psychomotor) | A1-A5 (Affective)
  ga_code      — e.g. 'GA1', 'GA2', 'GA-3' (looked up by ga_number within course's program; optional)

Reference columns (auto-filled from Courses, not stored):
  course_name, semester, course_class, credit_hours, pre_req, co_req

Each course typically has 3 CLOs. Theory and Lab are separate course_codes (e.g. CMC111 and CMC111-L).
Permanently deletes ALL CLOs.

List all Graduate Attributes. Accessible by Admin and Teacher.

Each GA in the response includes:
  id          — database ID (use this as mapped_ga when creating CLO via API)
  ga_number   — integer number (1, 2, 3...)
  ga_code     — string code 'GA-1', 'GA-2' etc. (use for dropdown display and CLO ga_code field)
  name        — GA name
  mapped_plos — list of PLO numbers mapped to this GA
Filter GAs for a specific program.

Admin only. mapped_plos = list of PLO IDs. GA names per picture: Individual and Teamwork, Communication, Computing Professionalism and Society, Ethics, Life-long Learning (GA 1-5 then repeat for GA 6-10 mapping to different PLOs).
Update mapped PLOs or any GA field partially.
Delete multiple GAs by ID list. Body: {"ids": [1, 2, 3]}

Admin only. Multipart form: file (CSV/XLSX). Columns: program_code, ga_number, name, description, mapped_plos (e.g. "PLO-1, PLO-2"). Standard GA names: Individual and Teamwork | Communication | Computing Professionalism and Society | Ethics | Life-long Learning
Permanently deletes ALL GAs from the database. Admin only.

Upload a course document (PDF / PPTX / TXT). The system will:
1. Extract text from the file.
2. Call Gemini to summarize key topics and concepts.
3. Store the summary — it is automatically injected into all paper/quiz/assignment generations for this course.

Required: course_id, title, file
Optional: doc_type — SLIDES (default), OUTLINE, OTHER

Teacher must be assigned to the course.
List uploaded documents. Filter by ?course=<id>. Admin sees all; Teacher sees only their assigned course documents.
Retrieve a single course document by ID.
Delete a course document and remove the file from disk. Teacher (own courses) or Admin only.

request.description:
Generate exam paper PDF.

Required:
  theory_course_id  — ID of the theory course (course_type=THEORY)
  topic             — exam topic (must be correctly spelled and relevant to the selected CLOs/PLOs)
  clo_ids           — list of CLO IDs (theory CLOs required; lab CLOs auto-fetched if lab course provided but none selected)
  plo_ids           — list of PLO IDs

Optional:
  lab_course_id        — ID of the lab course (course_type=LAB); omit if no lab course
  term                 — 'MIDTERM' or 'FINAL'. Displayed in PDF title: 'Midterm Examination Paper – Fall 2026'. Omit for plain 'Examination Paper'.
  mcq_count            — number of MCQs in Section A (default 5)
  short_q_count        — number of short questions in Section B (default 4)
  long_q_count         — number of long questions in Section C (default 3)
  programming_language — e.g. 'Python', 'Java', 'C++'. Required if course is programming-related.
  teacher_name         — defaults to logged-in teacher name
  total_marks          — default '60'; marks distributed proportionally across sections
  total_time           — default '2 Hours and 30 Minutes'

PDF title format: [Term] Examination Paper – [Semester Name from semesters API]
PDF header table:
  Course Code & Title  (full width)
  Program / Department (full width)
  Teacher's Name | Total Marks
  Date & Time    | Total Time
  Student's Name | Reg. No.

Topic validation (HTTP 422): topic must be correctly spelled and relevant to CLOs/PLOs.

List generated exam papers. Admin sees all; Teacher sees only papers for their assigned courses.

Each record includes:
  course_component     — 'THEORY + LAB' | 'THEORY' | 'LAB'
  theory_course_code/name/type — primary (theory) course details
  lab_course_code/name — paired lab course details (null if none)

Optional query params:
  ?course=<id>         — filter by a specific course ID (theory or lab)
  ?status=COMPLETED    — filter by status: COMPLETED or FAILED
  ?course_type=THEORY  — show only papers for theory-only courses
  ?course_type=LAB     — show only papers that include a lab course
Retrieve a single generated exam paper by ID.
Download the generated exam paper PDF file.
Delete a generated exam paper and its PDF file from disk.

Generate a quiz PDF for a single assigned course. Teacher only.

Required: course_id, topic, clo_ids, plo_ids
Optional:
  term             — 'MIDTERM' or 'FINAL'. Shown in PDF title.
  question_count   — total number of questions to generate (default 10)
  programming_language — e.g. 'Python', 'Java', 'C++'. Required if course is programming-related.
  teacher_name, total_marks (default 10), total_time (default 15 Minutes)

Topic validation (HTTP 422): topic must be correctly spelled and relevant to the selected CLOs and PLOs.

List generated quizzes. Admin sees all; Teacher sees only quizzes for their assigned courses.

Each record includes:
  course_component — 'THEORY' if generated for a theory course, 'LAB' if generated for a lab course
  theory_course_code/name/type — the course the quiz was generated for

Optional query params:
  ?course=<id>        — filter by a specific course ID
  ?status=COMPLETED   — filter by status: COMPLETED or FAILED
  ?course_type=THEORY — show only quizzes for theory courses
  ?course_type=LAB    — show only quizzes for lab courses
Retrieve a single generated quiz.
Download the generated quiz PDF.
Delete a generated quiz. Admin only.

Generate an assignment PDF for a single assigned course. Teacher only.

Required: course_id, topic, clo_ids, plo_ids
Optional:
  term             — 'MIDTERM' or 'FINAL'. Shown in PDF title.
  question_count   — number of assignment questions to generate (default 5)
  programming_language — e.g. 'Python', 'Java', 'C++'. Required if course is programming-related.
  teacher_name, total_marks (default 20), total_time (default 1 Hour)

Topic validation (HTTP 422): topic must be correctly spelled and relevant to the selected CLOs and PLOs.

List generated assignments. Admin sees all; Teacher sees only assignments for their assigned courses.

Each record includes:
  course_component — 'THEORY' if generated for a theory course, 'LAB' if generated for a lab course
  theory_course_code/name/type — the course the assignment was generated for

Optional query params:
  ?course=<id>        — filter by a specific course ID
  ?status=COMPLETED   — filter by status: COMPLETED or FAILED
  ?course_type=THEORY — show only assignments for theory courses
  ?course_type=LAB    — show only assignments for lab courses
Retrieve a single generated assignment.
Download the generated assignment PDF.
Delete a generated assignment. Admin only.
